summary: PR 1949 improved data matching in src/melee/un/un_2FC91.c by splitting a mixed float table so the heart icon values became their own static float array at 0x803F991C, updating users to index that new array, correcting a nametag rendering coordinate constant from 5000.0f to -5000.0f, and rewriting repeated NULL assignments as a PL_SLOT_MAX loop with PAD_STACK to preserve matching. It also refined GALE01 symbols and split ownership, moving the data range 0x803F99C8-0x803F9A00 from un_2FC91.c to un_2FC93.c and adding more precise object/string symbol boundaries.
what_changed: Changed static float un_803F98B8 from a 6x5 table to a 5x5 table, removing the heart icon row from that aggregate.
Added static float un_803F991C[4] at 0x803F991C containing heart icon values 24.0, 25.0, 26.0, and 27.0.
Updated un_802FC9B4 so the heart/team special case returns un_803F991C[Player_GetPlayerId(0)] instead of un_803F98B8[5][Player_GetPlayerId(0)].
Changed an HSD_SisLib_803A746C call in fn_802FCC44 from 5000.0f to -5000.0f for the third float argument.
Replaced six explicit un_804A1EE0[index] = NULL assignments in un_802FD4C8 with PAD_STACK(0x10) plus a for loop over PL_SLOT_MAX.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to shrink un_803F98B8 from size 0x74 to 0x64 and add un_803F991C at 0x803F991C.
Refined data symbols around 0x803F9940-0x803F99C8: un_803F9940 became size 0x14, un_803F9954 was added as size 0x40, and string labels lbl_803F9994, lbl_803F99AC, and lbl_803F99B8 were added.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt so un_2FC91.c data ends at 0x803F99C8 instead of 0x803F9A00, and un_2FC93.c now owns data from 0x803F99C8 to 0x803F9A00.
smart_moves: Separated the heart icon row into its own 4-element float array rather than forcing it into a rectangular 6x5 table, which better matches the actual data layout and symbol size at 0x803F991C.
Used the new un_803F991C symbol at the only shown call site that needed heart-specific indexing, making the code reflect the data boundary rather than relying on an artificial sixth row.
Added specific string labels for previously lumped data near 0x803F9994, improving future disassembly/data attribution and reducing oversized anonymous blobs.
Moved 0x803F99C8-0x803F9A00 split ownership to un_2FC93.c, aligning splits with symbol boundaries instead of leaving unrelated data under un_2FC91.c.
Used PAD_STACK(0x10) with a loop rewrite, suggesting the author preserved stack/codegen behavior while making repeated initialization more idiomatic.
decomp_lessons: Do not keep unrelated data in an over-large array just to satisfy a nearby reference; if the binary layout indicates a separate object, split it and update references.

Array dimensions should be guided by symbol size and actual access patterns. Here, a 6x5 float table was reduced to 5x5 and a separate 4-float table because the final row was not structurally the same object.
When splitting data symbols, update both source declarations and config/GALE01/symbols.txt sizes in the same change so the data map remains consistent.
Split-file ownership in config/GALE01/splits.txt should follow real data boundaries; moving a trailing data range to the neighboring unit can improve data matches without changing behavior.
Loop refactors in matching code may need explicit PAD_STACK to maintain compiler output, especially when replacing unrolled assignments.
Float sign errors can affect matching and behavior; even a single constant such as 5000.0f versus -5000.0f is worth checking against the target assembly/dataflow.
naming_conventions: Unknown data symbols in this area keep address-based names such as un_803F98B8, un_803F991C, un_803F9940, and un_803F9954.
Anonymous string data was labeled with lbl_ address names such as lbl_803F9994, lbl_803F99AC, and lbl_803F99B8.
Known semantic local/static names are used when understood, such as nametag_eyepos.
Player-slot constants use existing project naming such as PL_SLOT_MAX.
Stack padding uses the PAD_STACK macro rather than ad hoc dummy locals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split a mixed float data block into two static arrays to match object sizes and addresses: un_803F98B8 at 0x803F98B8 size 0x64 and un_803F991C at 0x803F991C size 0x10.
Adjusted symbols.txt sizes and inserted labels so the linker/map no longer treats several distinct objects/strings as one large blob.
Changed split boundaries so un_2FC91.c data stops at 0x803F99C8 and un_2FC93.c begins owning data at that address.
Used PAD_STACK(0x10) to keep generated code matching after converting explicit repeated NULL stores into a for loop.
Corrected a float literal sign in an HSD_SisLib_803A746C call, likely to align with observed assembly constants or runtime behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the supplied PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human text excerpt is empty, so no concrete reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Check whether other un_2FC91 data arrays still combine semantically different objects only to fit a rectangular declaration.
Search for remaining oversized un_803F99xx symbols that could be split into object and string labels.
Compare un_2FC93.c references to data at 0x803F99C8-0x803F9A00 after the split ownership move.
Review nearby HSD_SisLib_803A746C calls for similar float sign or coordinate mismatches.
Look for repeated NULL initialization patterns in matching-sensitive functions where a loop plus PAD_STACK may improve source quality without breaking matches.
